---
title: "Choosing A Method"
---

# Purpose

This note will help readers move from a policy question and data situation to a defensible quasi-experimental design choice.

# Decision Guide

## Start With The Assignment Process

Ask:

- Is treatment assigned using an explicit threshold?
- Does treatment start at different times across units?
- Is there one main treated unit and a donor pool?
- Is selection driven mainly by observed characteristics?

## First-Pass Mapping

- Use matching or weighting when the key problem is non-random selection on observed pre-treatment covariates.
- Use difference-in-differences when treatment varies over time and untreated units can anchor the counterfactual trend.
- Use synthetic control when a treated unit needs a transparent weighted comparator built from untreated units.
- Use regression discontinuity when assignment changes at a known threshold and local comparison is credible.

## Questions To Resolve Before Committing

- What is the estimand?
- What assumptions are doing the real work?
- What diagnostics would most likely falsify the design?
- Does the design answer the policy question for the right population?

# Planned Extension

This page should eventually include a short flowchart and worked examples of method choice under common evaluation scenarios.
